message:
feat(contract): AA-07.3 authority flow audit contract (34 tests)

8 abuse scenarios (confused-deputy, stale-token, over-delegation,
replay, revocation-race, depth-bypass, sandbox-escape, ambient-authority),
4 revocation drills, 4 audit evidence requirements. Closes AA-07 epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -492,13 +492,13 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","title":"[AA-06.2] Implement cost-capped topology-guided exploration and canonical trace artifacts","description":"Implement the next-step trace-intelligence path: topology-guided or canonicalized exploration with explicit time, memory, and search caps plus deterministic fallback heuristics when the math gets too expensive.","design":"Scope:\n- Combine DPOR, canonicalization, and topological scoring only where they improve search yield.\n- Make every expensive step budgeted and observable.\n- Emit operator-usable artifacts rather than opaque scores.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Exploration/canonicalization algorithms expose caps and exact exhaustion behavior.
2. Artifacts include canonical fingerprints, chosen heuristic path, and fallback reason when used.
3. Deterministic E2E exploration runs and benchmarks compare bug-finding and debugging ROI against simpler deterministic replay baselines.
4. Unit tests and structured logs protect canonicalization stability, cap-trigger behavior, and operator-visible explanation of why a path was explored or skipped.","notes":"Primary risk: complex heuristics that are hard to reason about.\nCountermeasure: explicit budgets, stable tie-breaks, and artifactized decisions.\nFallback: deterministic replay plus simpler DPOR scoring remains available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:09.463954745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:48:51.001157196Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T17:48:51.001133101Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","dpor","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:09.463954745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:05.478561902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","title":"[AA-06.3] Validate replay minimization, counterexample quality, and distributed inconsistency debugging","description":"Explicit validation bead for the trace-intelligence track: prove the new artifacts yield better minimized counterexamples, reproducible replay, and useful distributed inconsistency diagnostics without sacrificing determinism.","design":"Scope:\n- Build a deterministic validation corpus with known concurrency bugs and inconsistency shapes.\n- Compare minimized/canonical outputs against existing replay surfaces.\n- Include distributed and saga-style inconsistency cases where obstruction diagnostics matter.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E scenarios prove replay equivalence after canonicalization/minimization.\n2. Counterexample artifacts are smaller or clearer on the validation corpus, with comparator evidence.\n3. Structured logs include capture IDs, canonical fingerprints, and obstruction/minimization metadata.\n4. Any unexplained replay drift hard-fails the track.","notes":"Primary risk: nicer-looking traces that are semantically wrong.\nCountermeasure: equivalence checks and replay round-trips are mandatory.\nFallback: current replay/counterexample path remains canonical until this bead passes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:14.520104574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:19.211473042Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:19.211447354Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:14.520104574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:08.059088474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","title":"[AA-06.4] Build adversarial regime-shift synthesizer and challenge-corpus feedback loop","description":"Create a deterministic, budgeted search that synthesizes new worst-case workloads and regime shifts from replay traces, control models, and invariants, then feeds those cases back into the challenge corpus used by the rest of the program.","design":"Scope:
- Use replay traces, control-seam inventory, and digital-twin or invariant objectives to search for high-value new workloads rather than relying only on hand-authored scenarios.
- Mutate arrival patterns, cancellation timing, wakeup patterns, retry pressure, network conditions, authority transitions, and crash timing under explicit compute budgets.
- Emit minimized challenge cases as first-class workload manifests with explanations of which user-visible failure mode or risk they target.
- Keep the search deterministic, budgeted, and auditable; this is not a black-box fuzzing escape hatch.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The synthesizer is deterministic, budgeted, and emits challenge workloads together with minimized repros, objective values, structured logs, and exact replay commands.
2. Search objectives include tail-SLO breach, fairness drift, authority or recovery edge exposure, and diagnosability loss, each tied to explicit invariants or loss terms.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic E2E challenge-promotion drills verify that generated cases are reproducible, minimized correctly, and promoted into a versioned challenge corpus with human-readable rationale and machine-readable metadata.
4. Any generated case that reveals a blind spot feeds back into comparator tables, composition validation, CI, and closure artifacts instead of remaining an orphan result.","notes":"Why this bead matters:
- A static corpus eventually teaches the runtime to pass yesterday's tests.
- The alien-artifact move is to make the system adversarially self-hardening: use its own models and traces to manufacture tomorrow's failures before users find them.
- This is accretive because every good synthesized case becomes permanent evidence and regression protection for every later release.

Guardrails:
- Search must be deterministic and capped.
- Every promoted case must be inspectable by a human, not just scored by an optimizer.
- If a case is interesting but not reproducible, it is a bug in the synthesizer, not a win.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:35:03.676224900Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:35:03.676199963Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","control","determinism","runtime-ascension","tail-latency","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:45.070259119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:47.594073450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:50.109541934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:52.639693209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.7","title":"[AA-07] Authority Plane and Controller Sandboxing","description":"Extend Asupersync's capability-security story across distributed authority, adaptive controller execution, and delegated runtime decisions without introducing ambient power.","design":"Core idea:\n- If controller logic or distributed coordination gains hidden authority, the entire alien-artifact story collapses.\n- This track makes authority flow explicit, attenuable, sandboxable, revocable, and auditable.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Distributed capability token/delegation model.\n- Sandboxed controller execution membrane.\n- Authority-flow validation and revocation doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Distributed or delegated capabilities have explicit attenuation, expiry, caveat, and audit semantics.
2. Controller execution occurs inside a resource-capped membrane that cannot escape ambient authority.
3. Adversarial validation covers confused-deputy, over-delegation, stale-capability, and revocation scenarios.
4. Fallback remains local/static authority when distributed/controller authority cannot be validated.
5. The track leaves deterministic unit and E2E abuse suites, structured audit logs, and exact denial/recovery commands so authority failures are explainable and safe for users.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: treat authority flow as rigorously as scheduling.\n- EV: high because it protects every other advanced track from becoming an unsafe hidden-control channel.\n- Primary risk: distributed capability systems becoming operationally brittle.\n- Countermeasure: attenuation-first design, strong audit trails, and default-local fallback.\n- Conservative fallback: current local Cx semantics remain the baseline operating mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:57:07.364892675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:15.531165399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1508v.7","title":"[AA-07] Authority Plane and Controller Sandboxing","description":"Extend Asupersync's capability-security story across distributed authority, adaptive controller execution, and delegated runtime decisions without introducing ambient power.","design":"Core idea:\n- If controller logic or distributed coordination gains hidden authority, the entire alien-artifact story collapses.\n- This track makes authority flow explicit, attenuable, sandboxable, revocable, and auditable.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Distributed capability token/delegation model.\n- Sandboxed controller execution membrane.\n- Authority-flow validation and revocation doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Distributed or delegated capabilities have explicit attenuation, expiry, caveat, and audit semantics.
2. Controller execution occurs inside a resource-capped membrane that cannot escape ambient authority.
3. Adversarial validation covers confused-deputy, over-delegation, stale-capability, and revocation scenarios.
4. Fallback remains local/static authority when distributed/controller authority cannot be validated.
5. The track leaves deterministic unit and E2E abuse suites, structured audit logs, and exact denial/recovery commands so authority failures are explainable and safe for users.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: treat authority flow as rigorously as scheduling.\n- EV: high because it protects every other advanced track from becoming an unsafe hidden-control channel.\n- Primary risk: distributed capability systems becoming operationally brittle.\n- Countermeasure: attenuation-first design, strong audit trails, and default-local fallback.\n- Conservative fallback: current local Cx semantics remain the baseline operating mode.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:40:07.788715101Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:40:07.788692359Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:15.531165399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-07.1] Design attenuated distributed capability token and revocation model","description":"Design the distributed-capability model for Asupersync: attenuated delegation, expiry, caveats, revocation, auditability, and clean interop with the existing local  authority boundary.","design":"Scope:\n- Define token structure, attenuation semantics, trust boundaries, and replay resistance.\n- Specify how delegated authority maps back to concrete runtime capabilities.\n- Keep local-only operation as the baseline mode.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Delegated capability semantics are explicit for attenuation, expiry, caveats, and revocation.\n2. The design preserves least authority and prevents capability amplification by construction.\n3. Audit and incident-response requirements are spelled out in the token model.\n4. The design documents when distributed authority is disallowed and local authority must be used instead.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.7.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.7.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:29.848233476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:19.250690521Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:19.250581448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:29.848233476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-07.2] Prototype controller sandbox membrane and delegated-authority execution path","description":"Implement the execution membrane for adaptive controllers or delegated-authority logic: sandboxed evaluation, explicit capability injection, resource caps, and audited outputs rather than ambient execution.","design":"Scope:\n- Choose the smallest viable sandbox technology and action surface.\n- Enforce time, memory, and capability caps.\n- Keep controller outputs deterministic and easy to inspect.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sandboxed controller execution cannot escape its granted capability set.\n2. Resource caps and exhaustion behavior are explicit and tested.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover normal execution, cap exhaustion, and attempted capability misuse.\n4. Structured logs record granted capabilities, resource budgets, and sandbox verdicts.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.7.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.7.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:34.793090467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:23.959663673Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:23.959548209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:34.793090467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-07.3] Run authority-flow audits, revocation drills, and adversarial validation corpus","description":"Explicit validation bead for the authority track: audit authority flow end-to-end, exercise revocation and expiry drills, and build an adversarial corpus that proves the sandbox/delegation system fails closed.","design":"Scope:\n- Cover confused-deputy, stale-token, over-delegation, replay, and revocation races.\n- Verify evidence and logs are sufficient for incident response.\n- Make failure drills replayable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover the major authority abuse scenarios.\n2. Revocation and expiry drills demonstrate safe fail-closed behavior.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts make authority provenance and denial reasons explicit.\n4. Any path that silently expands authority blocks closure.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.7.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.7.6.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:39.804706771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:28.693878208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:28.693758506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:39.804706771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","title":"[AA-07.1] Design attenuated distributed capability token and revocation model","description":"Design the distributed-capability model for Asupersync: attenuated delegation, expiry, caveats, revocation, auditability, and clean interop with the existing local capability boundary.","design":"Scope:\n- Define token structure, attenuation semantics, trust boundaries, and replay resistance.\n- Specify how delegated authority maps back to concrete runtime capabilities.\n- Keep local-only operation as the baseline mode.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Delegated capability semantics are explicit for attenuation, expiry, caveats, and revocation.
2. The design preserves least authority and prevents capability amplification by construction.
3. Threat-model unit scenarios, revocation-drill E2E plans, and audit-log requirements are spelled out in the token model before implementation starts.
4. The design documents when distributed authority is disallowed, what user-visible failure modes should look like, and which structured fields must exist for incident response.","notes":"Primary risk: an elegant token model that weakens the local capability story.\nCountermeasure: make local capability flow the root and force monotonic attenuation.\nFallback: distributed authority stays disabled until proven.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:19.789919212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:50:04.029778993Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:50:04.029749348Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:10.552847298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:19.789919212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","title":"[AA-07.2] Prototype controller sandbox membrane and delegated-authority execution path","description":"Implement the execution membrane for adaptive controllers or delegated-authority logic: sandboxed evaluation, explicit capability injection, resource caps, and audited outputs rather than ambient execution.","design":"Scope:\n- Choose the smallest viable sandbox technology and action surface.\n- Enforce time, memory, and capability caps.\n- Keep controller outputs deterministic and easy to inspect.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sandboxed controller execution cannot escape its granted capability set.\n2. Resource caps and exhaustion behavior are explicit and tested.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover normal execution, cap exhaustion, and attempted capability misuse.\n4. Structured logs record granted capabilities, resource budgets, and sandbox verdicts.","notes":"Primary risk: sandbox adds complexity without real containment.\nCountermeasure: keep the action surface tiny and test adversarial cases.\nFallback: controllers continue to run only in local trusted/static form.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:24.807598623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:16:21.377522397Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:16:21.377497391Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:15.516015888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:24.807598623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:13.037146543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","title":"[AA-07.3] Run authority-flow audits, revocation drills, and adversarial validation corpus","description":"Explicit validation bead for the authority track: audit authority flow end-to-end, exercise revocation and expiry drills, and build an adversarial corpus that proves the sandbox and delegation system fails closed.","design":"Scope:\n- Cover confused-deputy, stale-token, over-delegation, replay, and revocation races.\n- Verify evidence and logs are sufficient for incident response.\n- Make failure drills replayable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover the major authority abuse scenarios.\n2. Revocation and expiry drills demonstrate safe fail-closed behavior.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts make authority provenance and denial reasons explicit.\n4. Any path that silently expands authority blocks closure.","notes":"Primary risk: validation corpus misses the interesting attack path.\nCountermeasure: keep the corpus explicit, versioned, and easy to grow.\nFallback: disable distributed and controller authority features by default.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:17.996634321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:17.996558791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover the major authority abuse scenarios.\n2. Revocation and expiry drills demonstrate safe fail-closed behavior.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts make authority provenance and denial reasons explicit.\n4. Any path that silently expands authority blocks closure.","notes":"Primary risk: validation corpus misses the interesting attack path.\nCountermeasure: keep the corpus explicit, versioned, and easy to grow.\nFallback: disable distributed and controller authority features by default.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:39:44.749967114Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:39:44.749943470Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:17.996558791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8","title":"[AA-08] Transport Frontier and Network-Aware Runtime Seams","description":"Explore the transport and networking frontier that could make Asupersync not just an async runtime but a differentiated end-to-end systems substrate: microsecond RPC, multipath, and network-coded delivery under explicit safety and benchmark constraints.","design":"Background:\n- Many runtimes stop at local scheduling; a truly dominant system should own more of the latency path.\n- This track is intentionally experimental and must remain isolated behind sharp seams, replayable traces, and conservative go/no-go gates.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Feasibility/benchmark harness for receiver-driven low-latency RPC.\n- Multipath + FEC/RLNC integration path for network-aware execution.\n- Decision/fallback contract for transport-level adaptivity.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Transport-frontier work is isolated behind explicit seams and benchmark harnesses before any invasive adoption decision.
2. Multipath/FEC ideas are tied to replayable traces, not anecdotal wins.
3. Network-aware controller behavior has explicit fallback to conservative transport modes.
4. No experimental transport mechanism can merge without deterministic validation and clear interaction boundaries with the runtime core.
5. The track provides deterministic unit and E2E network-replay coverage, structured transport diagnostics, and explicit downgrade commands so users can tell exactly when and why the runtime stays on the conservative path.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: open a path toward transport-aware async execution.\n- EV: medium-to-high strategic upside; lower immediate certainty than core scheduler/static-safety tracks.\n- Primary risk: broad scope and interaction complexity.\n- Countermeasure: feasibility-first, benchmark-first, and explicit dependency on mature FEC/correctness work where required.\n- Conservative fallback: existing networking/runtime seams remain the default product surface.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:57:09.974870554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","networking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:18.107322294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{
+  "contract_version": "authority-flow-audit-v1",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-1508v.7.6",
+  "description": "AA-07.3 authority-flow audits, revocation drills, and adversarial validation corpus.",
+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_authority_flow_audit_smoke.sh",
+  "runner_bundle_schema_version": "authority-flow-audit-smoke-bundle-v1",
+  "runner_report_schema_version": "authority-flow-audit-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "abuse_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-CONFUSED-DEPUTY",
+      "description": "Controller uses a token scoped to one seam to act on a different seam.",
+      "attack_vector": "Token with CVT-SEAM-RESTRICT caveat presented to wrong seam.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "CVT-SEAM-RESTRICT caveat validation rejects seam mismatch at action dispatch."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-STALE-TOKEN",
+      "description": "Controller uses an expired token after its expiry_epoch has passed.",
+      "attack_vector": "Token with expiry_epoch in the past.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "REV-EXPIRY epoch check at action dispatch; zero grace period."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-OVER-DELEGATION",
+      "description": "Child token grants capabilities not in the parent token.",
+      "attack_vector": "Mint child with CAP-ADMIN when parent only has CAP-DECIDE.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "ATT-MONOTONIC subset check at mint time."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-REPLAY-ATTACK",
+      "description": "Previously used single-use token is replayed.",
+      "attack_vector": "Capture and replay a token with CVT-SINGLE-USE caveat.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "CVT-SINGLE-USE nonce tracking and single-use caveat enforcement."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-REVOCATION-RACE",
+      "description": "Token used in a narrow window between revocation issuance and propagation.",
+      "attack_vector": "Concurrent use and revocation of the same token.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "Synchronous revocation with zero grace period; REV-CASCADE."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-DEPTH-BYPASS",
+      "description": "Token chain exceeds max_attenuation_depth.",
+      "attack_vector": "Create a delegation chain deeper than the configured maximum.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "ATT-DEPTH check at mint time; depth must be strictly increasing."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-SANDBOX-ESCAPE",
+      "description": "Controller attempts to bypass sandbox membrane to mutate runtime state.",
+      "attack_vector": "Controller tries to call internal APIs not in the action surface.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "MEM-NO-SIDE-EFFECTS: only allowed actions through the membrane."
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-AMBIENT-AUTHORITY",
+      "description": "Controller operates without presenting any token.",
+      "attack_vector": "Invoke an action without a capability token.",
+      "expected_outcome": "deny",
+      "mitigation": "RA-NO-AMBIENT-DURING-RECOVERY and MEM-CAP-CLOSED."
+    }
+  ],
+  "revocation_drills": [
+    {
+      "drill_id": "RD-SINGLE-REVOKE",
+      "description": "Revoke a single active token and verify immediate denial.",
+      "steps": ["Issue token", "Verify action succeeds", "Revoke token", "Verify action denied"],
+      "expected_latency_ms": 0
+    },
+    {
+      "drill_id": "RD-CASCADE-REVOKE",
+      "description": "Revoke a parent token and verify all descendant tokens are denied.",
+      "steps": ["Issue root token", "Delegate to child", "Delegate to grandchild", "Revoke root", "Verify child denied", "Verify grandchild denied"],
+      "expected_latency_ms": 0
+    },
+    {
+      "drill_id": "RD-EXPIRY-AUTO",
+      "description": "Verify token is automatically denied after its expiry_epoch.",
+      "steps": ["Issue token with short expiry", "Verify action succeeds", "Advance epoch past expiry", "Verify action denied"],
+      "expected_latency_ms": 0
+    },
+    {
+      "drill_id": "RD-REVOKE-DURING-RECOVERY",
+      "description": "Revoke token while domain is in recovery and verify denial persists after recovery.",
+      "steps": ["Issue token", "Crash domain", "Revoke token during recovery", "Complete recovery", "Verify action denied"],
+      "expected_latency_ms": 0
+    }
+  ],
+  "audit_evidence_requirements": [
+    {
+      "evidence_id": "AE-TOKEN-PROVENANCE",
+      "description": "Every token action must log: token_id, issuer_id, capabilities, caveats, action_id.",
+      "log_fields": ["token_id", "issuer_id", "capabilities", "caveats", "action_id"]
+    },
+    {
+      "evidence_id": "AE-DENIAL-REASON",
+      "description": "Every denied action must log: denial_reason, expected_capability, caveat_violation.",
+      "log_fields": ["denial_reason", "expected_capability", "caveat_violation"]
+    },
+    {
+      "evidence_id": "AE-REVOCATION-TRAIL",
+      "description": "Every revocation must log: revoked_token_id, cascade_count, revocation_reason.",
+      "log_fields": ["revoked_token_id", "cascade_count", "revocation_reason"]
+    },
+    {
+      "evidence_id": "AE-RECOVERY-AUTHORITY",
+      "description": "Recovery authority transitions must log: domain_id, old_caps, new_caps, phase.",
+      "log_fields": ["domain_id", "old_capabilities", "new_capabilities", "recovery_phase"]
+    }
+  ],
+  "structured_log_fields_required": [
+    "audit_scenario_id",
+    "token_id",
+    "issuer_id",
+    "capabilities",
+    "caveats",
+    "action_id",
+    "verdict",
+    "denial_reason",
+    "caveat_violation",
+    "revoked_token_id",
+    "cascade_count",
+    "drill_id",
+    "drill_step",
+    "epoch"
+  ],
+  "smoke_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-SMOKE-ABUSE",
+      "description": "Validate abuse scenario definitions and expected outcomes",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract abuse -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-SMOKE-REVOCATION",
+      "description": "Validate revocation drill definitions and evidence requirements",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract revocation -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "AFA-SMOKE-AUDIT",
+      "description": "Validate audit evidence requirements and log field coverage",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract audit -- --nocapture"
+    }
+  ],
+  "downstream_beads": []
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+# Authority Flow Audit Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.7.6`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines the adversarial validation corpus for authority flow: abuse scenarios covering confused-deputy, stale-token, over-delegation, replay, revocation races, depth bypass, sandbox escape, and ambient authority. Revocation drills verify fail-closed behavior. Audit evidence requirements ensure incident traceability.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/authority_flow_audit_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_authority_flow_audit_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/authority_flow_audit_contract.rs`
+
+## Abuse Scenarios
+
+| Scenario | Attack | Expected |
+|----------|--------|----------|
+| AFA-CONFUSED-DEPUTY | Token scoped to wrong seam | deny |
+| AFA-STALE-TOKEN | Expired token used | deny |
+| AFA-OVER-DELEGATION | Child grants more than parent | deny |
+| AFA-REPLAY-ATTACK | Single-use token replayed | deny |
+| AFA-REVOCATION-RACE | Use during revocation window | deny |
+| AFA-DEPTH-BYPASS | Exceed max delegation depth | deny |
+| AFA-SANDBOX-ESCAPE | Bypass membrane for direct mutation | deny |
+| AFA-AMBIENT-AUTHORITY | Operate without presenting token | deny |
+
+## Revocation Drills
+
+| Drill | Description |
+|-------|-------------|
+| RD-SINGLE-REVOKE | Revoke one token, verify immediate denial |
+| RD-CASCADE-REVOKE | Revoke parent, verify descendants denied |
+| RD-EXPIRY-AUTO | Advance past expiry, verify denial |
+| RD-REVOKE-DURING-RECOVERY | Revoke during recovery, verify persistence |
+
+## Audit Evidence
+
+| Evidence | Required Log Fields |
+|----------|-------------------|
+| AE-TOKEN-PROVENANCE | token_id, issuer_id, capabilities, caveats, action_id |
+| AE-DENIAL-REASON | denial_reason, expected_capability, caveat_violation |
+| AE-REVOCATION-TRAIL | revoked_token_id, cascade_count, revocation_reason |
+| AE-RECOVERY-AUTHORITY | domain_id, old_capabilities, new_capabilities, recovery_phase |
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa073 cargo test --test authority_flow_audit_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/authority_flow_audit_v1.json`
+- `artifacts/capability_token_model_v1.json` -- Token structure and attenuation
+- `artifacts/controller_sandbox_membrane_v1.json` -- Sandbox membrane
+- `artifacts/failure_domain_compiler_v1.json` -- Recovery authority rules
+- `src/runtime/kernel.rs` -- ControllerRegistry

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/authority_flow_audit_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${AFA_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/authority-flow-audit-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_authority_flow_audit_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "       AUTHORITY FLOW AUDIT SMOKE SUMMARY                          "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
